overall.description: A maritime simulation game depicting nighttime navigation from a ship's bridge. The vessel moves steadily through a harbor channel while navigation displays show radar scanning and chart data, with distant shore lights visible in the darkness.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge with navigation consoles overlooking the bow

agents.ego_description: piloting the ship from the bridge, monitoring the rotating radar display and nautical charts while navigating through the harbor channel at night

entered.detail: nautical chart display showing coastal geography with colored depth contours and ship position marker
circular radar display with rotating yellow scan beam showing channel boundaries and navigation hazards
blue digital display showing depth sounder data and navigation parameters
triangular bow structure of the vessel visible through the forward bridge windows
metal safety railing at the front of the ship deck
dark calm water surface of the harbor navigation channel
distant city lights visible on the port side shoreline
distant industrial lights visible on the starboard side shoreline

segments.scene_description: The view from a ship's bridge at night shows the vessel moving steadily through a harbor channel. Three navigation displays dominate the upper frame - a chart on the left, a rotating radar in the center, and depth data on the right. Through the forward windows, the ship's bow cuts through dark water toward distant shore lights, while the radar continuously scans the surroundings for navigation hazards.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot maintains a steady forward perspective from a ship's bridge as the vessel navigates through a dark harbor channel toward distant shore lights. It begins with a focus on the bridge's instrumentation, where three navigation displays showing a chart, a rotating radar, and depth data dominate the upper frame. As the ship moves forward, the camera looks through the forward windows to see the bow cutting through the dark water. The scene then emphasizes the continuous scanning of the radar as it monitors the surroundings for hazards while the vessel draws closer to the flickering lights of the distant shoreline.
